task_completion.description: Import the dawn chorus recording into Ardour
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Ardour timeline shows an audio track containing the imported 'dawn_chorus_recording' waveform.

task_completion.description: Split the audio into five segments at 6-second intervals
task_completion.visual_evidence: The audio track in Ardour is visibly split into exactly 5 distinct regions, with split points aligning with 0:06.000, 0:12.000, 0:18.000, and 0:24.000 on the timeline ruler.
The text labels on the audio regions in the Ardour timeline read 'BKGD-ambient-01', 'AMRO-vocalization', 'MOCH-vocalization', 'YWAR-vocalization', and 'BKGD-ambient-02' from left to right.

The first ('BKGD-ambient-01') and fifth ('BKGD-ambient-02') regions on the timeline appear greyed out, dimmed, or have a visible mute indicator, distinguishing them from the active vocalization regions.

task_completion.description: Add range markers for the three bird species
task_completion.visual_evidence: The marker/range track at the top of the Ardour timeline displays three distinct range markers labeled 'AMRO - American Robin' (spanning 0:06-0:12), 'MOCH - Mountain Chickadee' (0:12-0:18), and 'YWAR - Yellow Warbler' (0:18-0:24).

task_completion.description: Record the species codes in a text log file
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor or terminal window displays the contents of 'species_log.txt' containing the exact codes 'AMRO', 'MOCH', and 'YWAR'.

integrity.description: The agent used the Ardour GUI to perform the audio editing tasks
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the Ardour application window open with active interaction (mouse clicks, menus, timeline edits), rather than terminal commands executing scripts to modify the session.

integrity.description: The agent did not bypass the GUI by directly editing the Ardour session XML file
integrity.visual_evidence: There should be no screenshots showing a text editor open with the raw XML code of the '.ardour' session file being manually modified to insert regions or markers.

integrity.description: The agent genuinely created or edited the species log file
integrity.visual_evidence: The agent is seen opening a text editor or using standard terminal commands (like echo or nano) to write the species codes into 'species_log.txt'.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The audio file is exactly 30 seconds long. It must be split into exactly 5 segments at 0:06.000, 0:12.000, 0:18.000, and 0:24.000. The species codes to be logged are AMRO, MOCH, and YWAR. The background regions to be muted are the first (0-6s) and last (24-30s) segments.
